main_sections.title: Gone with the Scooter
main_sections.prose_summary: On a hot summer afternoon, Gopi is reading on the veranda when he hears a rustling and a thud in the garden. He searches through the grass, bushes, and dry leaves and finds a hockey ball. He wonders whose it could be and decides he cannot keep it — he will ask around. That evening he goes to the nearby playground where his friends Manoj, Ramani, Deepak, and Jay have gathered. He asks if anyone lost a ball. Manoj says he lost one four months ago, but Gopi rules that out. Ramani tries to claim it without proof. Deepak boasts his father bought him a new football. Everyone laughs. Manoj grabs the ball and tosses it to Deepak, triggering a spontaneous game of hockey. Gopi passes to Deepak, who drives to Ramani, who dribbles to Jay. Jay hits the ball so hard it flies past the playground gate and lands in the basket of a passing scooter. By the time the children run to the gate, the scooter — and the ball — are gone. The friends burst into laughter. The story celebrates honesty, sharing, sportsmanship, and the joy of unexpected play, with a comic twist ending that mirrors the irony of the ball's journey: found by chance, lost again by chance.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Answer the following questions.
1. What was Gopi doing on the veranda?
2. What sound did Gopi hear before he went to the garden?
3. Describe Gopi's search for the ball.
4. Complete the table given below.
S.No. | Dialogue | The dialogue was said by | The dialogue was said to
a. The ball I found is a hockey ball.
b. My father bought me a brand-new football.
c. I lost a ball four months ago.
d. Show me the ball and I will tell you.
5. Who grabbed the ball from Gopi? How did the game start?
6. Why did everyone laugh at the end of the story?

question_bank.stem: What was Gopi doing on the veranda?
What sound did Gopi hear before he went to the garden?
Describe Gopi's search for the ball.

Complete the table: identify who said each dialogue and to whom. Dialogues: (a) The ball I found is a hockey ball. (b) My father bought me a brand-new football. (c) I lost a ball four months ago. (d) Show me the ball and I will tell you.
question_bank.table:
S.No.|Dialogue|The dialogue was said by|The dialogue was said to
a.|The ball I found is a hockey ball.||
b.|My father bought me a brand-new football.||
c.|I lost a ball four months ago.||
d.|Show me the ball and I will tell you.||

question_bank.stem: Who grabbed the ball from Gopi? How did the game start?
Why did everyone laugh at the end of the story?

oracy.section_name: Let us Think B + Let us Listen + Let us Speak A

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Think and answer.
1. What would you have done if, like Gopi, you had found a ball that did not belong to you?
2. Imagine what happens to the hockey ball after it is taken away by the scooter. Where does it go? Who finds it?
3. Notice that the scooterist is wearing a helmet. Why is it important to wear a helmet? Should the pillion rider also wear a helmet?

question_bank.stem: What would you have done if, like Gopi, you had found a ball that did not belong to you?
Imagine what happens to the hockey ball after it is taken away by the scooter. Where does it go? Who finds it?
Notice that the scooterist is wearing a helmet. Why is it important to wear a helmet? Should the pillion rider also wear a helmet?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Listen A. Listen to your teacher. Follow the instructions and draw accordingly in the space provided below.
• Draw a few big dry leaves at the centre with no gap between the leaves. Draw a ball in the middle of the leaves.
• Draw a few twigs here and there.
• Colour the leaves yellow and brown. Colour the twigs brown.
• Now you have found the ball.
tasks_verbatim.transcript_text: Teacher reads instructions aloud, one step at a time: Step 1 — Draw a few big dry leaves at the centre with no gap between the leaves; draw a ball in the middle of the leaves. Step 2 — Draw a few twigs here and there. Step 3 — Colour the leaves yellow and brown; colour the twigs brown. Step 4 — Announce: 'Now you have found the ball.' Students listen and draw in the space provided on p.19.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Speak A. The teacher starts a story with one sentence. Each student adds a sentence to continue the story.
For example:
Teacher: 'Once upon a time, a boy found a magic key.'
Student 1: 'The key opened a door to a secret garden.'
Student 2: 'In the garden, he saw a talking rabbit.'

word_work.section_name: Let us Learn A + Let us Learn B + Let us Learn C + Let us Speak B

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn A. Match the following.
Word — Meaning
Dribble — a soft crackling sound
Boast — take something with a sudden movement
Rustle — moving a ball with small taps while playing hockey
Grab — to speak too proudly

question_bank.stem: Match: Dribble → ?
Match: Boast → ?
Match: Rustle → ?
Match: Grab → ?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn B. Complete the following story using the words given in the box.
[Word bank: since, however, because, when]
Gopi completed his work ________ he wanted to go outside and play. ________ when he reached the playground nobody was there. ________ his friends were yet to join him, he decided to take a walk. After 15 minutes, five of them came and everyone started to discuss the games. They decided to play hopscotch ________ they got to know that Ramani would not be joining them.

question_bank.stem: Gopi completed his work ________ he wanted to go outside and play. [since/however/because/when]
________ when he reached the playground nobody was there. [since/however/because/when]
________ his friends were yet to join him, he decided to take a walk. [since/however/because/when]
They decided to play hopscotch ________ they got to know that Ramani would not be joining them. [since/however/because/when]

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn C. Now encircle the adverbs in the following sentences. For each sentence, identify whether the adverb tells 'how many times the action happens/happened' (frequency) or 'time of the action'.
a. Gopi immediately got up and ran into the garden.
b. Shama often writes in her diary.
c. All his friends regularly gathered to play hockey.
d. I always complete my homework.
e. Yesterday I ate an ice cream after lunch.
f. Monika never eats junk food.
g. Now they are going to sing their favourite song.

question_bank.stem: Encircle the adverb and classify it (frequency/time): Gopi immediately got up and ran into the garden.
Encircle the adverb and classify it (frequency/time): Shama often writes in her diary.
Encircle the adverb and classify it (frequency/time): All his friends regularly gathered to play hockey.
Encircle the adverb and classify it (frequency/time): I always complete my homework.
Encircle the adverb and classify it (frequency/time): Yesterday I ate an ice cream after lunch.
Encircle the adverb and classify it (frequency/time): Monika never eats junk food.
Encircle the adverb and classify it (frequency/time): Now they are going to sing their favourite song.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Speak B. The title of this textbook is 'Santoor'. Say the word 'book'. Next, say the word 'Santoor'. Both have 'oo' in them — the sound of 'oo' in 'book' is short; the sound of 'oo' in 'Santoor' is long.
Say aloud the following words. Encircle the words which have a long 'oo' sound.
1. Hood  2. School  3. Foot  4. Maroon  5. Scooter  6. Shook  7. Bloom  8. Food  9. Look  10. Moon  11. Book  12. Took

question_bank.stem: Encircle words with a long 'oo' sound from the list: Hood, School, Foot, Maroon, Scooter, Shook, Bloom, Food, Look, Moon, Book, Took

writing.section_name: Let us Write A + Let us Write B + Let us Write C

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Write A. By the time the children ran to the gate, the scooter was gone… and so was the ball. They looked at each other and burst into laughter.
Write a possible conversation between Gopi and his friends after this incident.
Gopi: ______
Ramani: ______
Deepak: ______
Manoj: ______
Jay: ______

Let us Write B. Form new words by following the examples given below:
sun + rise = sunrise [also: sun + set = sunset; sun + shine = sunshine; sun + flower = sunflower]
foot + ball = football [also: foot + base = baseball; foot + basket = basketball; foot + volley = volleyball]
Write a sentence using each of these words in your notebook.

question_bank.stem: Form compound word: sun + set = ?
Form compound word: sun + shine = ?
Form compound word: sun + flower = ?
Form compound word: foot + base = ?
Form compound word: foot + basket = ?
Form compound word: foot + volley = ?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Write C. You have already noticed that the word 'scooter' has 'oo' in the middle. Using the clues given below, write ten words that have 'ai' in the middle. One has been done for you (chair).
1. Something that has four legs, on which you generally sit. (chair)
2. Water that falls from clouds.
3. A vehicle that runs on tracks.
4. You use this to colour your wall or pictures.
5. The part of body that helps you think.
6. A slow moving creature with a shell.
7. Letters and packages you send or receive.
8. The movements of boats on water.
9. All animals have them, long or short.
10. Steps that takes us from one floor to another.

question_bank.stem: Water that falls from clouds. (has 'ai' in the middle)
A vehicle that runs on tracks. (has 'ai' in the middle)
You use this to colour your wall or pictures. (has 'ai' in the middle)
The part of body that helps you think. (has 'ai' in the middle)
A slow moving creature with a shell. (has 'ai' in the middle)
Letters and packages you send or receive. (has 'ai' in the middle)
The movements of boats on water. (has 'ai' in the middle)
All animals have them, long or short. (has 'ai' in the middle)
Steps that takes us from one floor to another. (has 'ai' in the middle)

beyond_text.section_name: Let us Do + Let us Explore

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Do. Prepare a board game to play 'Tigers and Goats'.
1. Take a square piece of cardboard.
2. Paste a chart paper on it.
3. Draw the pattern as shown in the picture.
4. Collect two different types of pebbles — three big pebbles and thirteen smaller ones. Paint them red for tigers and green for goats.
How to play: At least two players needed — one 'Goat', one 'Tiger'. Tiger's objective: defeat 5 goats. Goat's objective: surround all three tigers so they cannot move. Both pebbles placed on intersecting points. The tiger may jump over the goat to capture it. Goats cannot move until all 13 are on the board.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: A triangular grid board game layout showing intersecting lines with red (tiger) and green (goat) pebble positions for the 'Tigers and Goats' game.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Explore A. Prepare a chart with the following categories about games you know: Name of the game | Outdoor/Indoor | Players needed | Equipment needed. (Example given: Kabaddi — outdoor — team — nil.) Fill in at least 9 more games.
Let us Explore B. Write a mini biography about a famous sports person of your choice using these hints: name, sport, when and where born, one or two achievements, why the person is famous/inspiring.
Let us Explore C. Find out about a famous hockey player and talk about them.

question_bank.stem: Prepare a chart about 10 games you know with columns: Name of the game | Outdoor/Indoor | Players needed | Equipment needed.
